name: Cystic Fibrosis
summary: Cystic fibrosis is caused by pathogenic CFTR variants affecting epithelial ion transport. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Manifests in childhood/adolescence; included as monogenic architecture example (educational only).

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS not applicable as primary model for monogenic CF.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Secondhand smoke can worsen respiratory burden; evidence varies by context.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: CF variant catalogs have been European-ancestry weighted historically; variant spectrum differs across populations.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Variant interpretation can be impacted by reference dataset representation.
population_equity.data_gaps: Environmental exposure datasets linked to CF outcomes are limited.

references.title: Cystic fibrosis: CFTR biology and clinical manifestations (review)
Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association

references.title: Infection exposure and immune-mediated disease mechanisms: overview (review)
The Health Consequences of Smoking—50 Years of Progress
references.journal: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
